draft.subject: RE: RE: Records Request - Clarification Needed
draft.body_text:
The incident date is March 15, 2024.

I do not have a DR number or case number at this time.

Location: 500 N Main St, Los Angeles, CA (near the intersection of N Main St and E Cesar E Chavez Ave).

If it would help speed things up, I’m also happy to narrow the request to records specifically referencing David Lee for that date and location (e.g., incident report, arrest report, and any related CAD/dispatch entries). Please let me know what scope is most practical for your search.

Thank you,

Requester

Thank you,

Requester

results.description: High fee quote requiring breakdown request

key_points: Estimated total cost is $750.00
Search: 5 hours at $75/hr
Review: 3 hours at $75/hr
Redaction: 1 hour at $75/hr
Agency asks to confirm proceeding
analysis.summary: NYPD provided a $750 cost estimate with an hourly breakdown and requests confirmation to proceed before continuing.

draft.subject: RE: Fee Response - Grace Kim
draft.body_text:
Hello,

Thanks for the update on request “Grace Kim” and the fee estimate of $750. To help bring the cost down, could we narrow the search to:

- Body-worn camera and any relevant vehicle video from the primary responding officer(s) and the arresting officer(s) only
- A tighter time window (for example, 30 minutes before through 30 minutes after the incident time)

Also, what information would be most helpful for you to identify the key footage quickly (for example, an incident/report number, arrest number, or CAD/event number)? If you tell me what you need, I can provide it.

Finally, we’d like to request a public interest/media fee waiver or reduction under the New York Freedom of Information Law, since the records will be used for news/documentary reporting and shared with the public.

Thank you,

Requester

results.description: Response mentions minors/sensitive content

Incident involves a 14-year-old minor victim
Body camera footage contains graphic injury descriptions

They ask if you want the redacted version
analysis.summary: Portland Police Bureau found responsive records but says the incident involves a juvenile and graphic content, so they will provide a heavily redacted version. They are asking you to confirm whether you want to proceed with the redacted footage.

draft.subject: RE: RE: Records Request - Sensitive Content Notice
draft.body_text:
Yes, please proceed with the redacted version of the body-worn camera footage, with faces, voices, and any identifying details about the juvenile obscured.

To make sure I’m requesting the correct records, could you confirm the incident date and location (or the case number)? If there are multiple responsive videos, I’m also happy to narrow to:
- A specific date/time window
- A specific officer’s camera
- Only the segments showing the primary incident (excluding extended medical/hospital portions)

If providing the redacted footage will involve any fees, please send an estimate before you begin.

Thank you.

Requester
draft.body_html:
Yes, please proceed with the redacted version of the body-worn camera footage, with faces, voices, and any identifying details about the juvenile obscured.

To make sure I’m requesting the correct records, could you confirm the incident date and location (or the case number)? If there are multiple responsive videos, I’m also happy to narrow to:
- A specific date/time window
- A specific officer’s camera
- Only the segments showing the primary incident (excluding extended medical/hospital portions)

If providing the redacted footage will involve any fees, please send an estimate before you begin.

Thank you.

Requester

results.description: First follow-up after no response (7 days)

draft.subject: Follow-up: Public Records Request - Mike Nelson
draft.body_text:
Hello,

I’m following up on my public records request submitted on 2026-01-13 regarding “Mike Nelson” to the Austin Police Department. I know requests can take time, so I wanted to check in and see if there’s an update on its status.

When you have a moment, could you please confirm whether it’s been received and share an estimated timeline for next steps?

We remain interested in obtaining the responsive records. If it would help move things along, I’m also happy to clarify the request or narrow the scope.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Requester

results.description: Second follow-up after no response (14 days)

draft.subject: Follow-up: Public Records Request - Nancy Green
draft.body_text:
Hello,

I’m following up on my Texas Public Information Act request sent on 2026-01-06 regarding records related to Nancy Green. I have not yet received a response, and I’m still awaiting an update.

Under Texas’s 10-business-day response deadline, a determination should have been issued. Please provide the current status and an estimated date for when responsive records (or a written determination) will be available.

If there are scope or clarification questions that are delaying processing, please let me know and I’m happy to work with you to narrow or clarify the request so it can be completed.

Thank you,

Requester

results.description: Final follow-up before escalation (21 days)

draft.subject: Follow-up: Public Records Request - Oscar Brown
draft.body_text:
Hello,

This is my final follow-up regarding my Texas Public Information Act request sent on 2025-12-31 to the Fort Worth Police Department concerning records related to Oscar Brown. I’m still awaiting any acknowledgement, tracking number, determination, or estimated completion date.

Texas generally requires a response within 10 business days. Please provide a written determination on the status of this request, including:
- Whether the department will release the requested records (in whole or in part)
- Any expected timeline for production
- If any portion is being withheld, the basis and what can be released now

If this request needs clarification or narrowing to speed processing, please let me know what options would be most helpful. If appropriate, please also share the contact information for the supervisor or public information officer overseeing this request.

Thank you.

Requester

draft.subject: RE: Fee Response - Robert Stone
draft.body_text:
Thanks for the estimate on the Robert Stone request. The $125.00 cost is a bit high for our budget, so can we narrow the scope to reduce the number of videos?

- Limit to body-worn/dash camera footage for the primary responding officer(s) and the arresting officer(s) only
- Limit the time window to 30 minutes before through 30 minutes after the incident/arrest (or the dispatch/arrival time, if easier)

If you tell me what identifiers you need to pull just the most relevant footage (incident number, report number, CAD/event number, exact address, date/time), I can provide what we have.

Also, please consider a fee waiver or reduction in the public interest under Pennsylvania’s Right-to-Know Law, 65 P.S. § 67.1307(f), as this is for news/documentary reporting.

Best regards, 

Requester
